events.description: Competency Commission reviewed cases for fee patents
Declaration of Policy period for releasing Indians from supervision
Secretary of Interior delivered 186 patents in fee upon Competency Commission report
Approximately 306 patents issued prior to 1916, many by special Act of Congress
Over 100 patents issued under Declaration Policy
Approximately 90% of patentees sold or mortgaged lands
Approximately 550 of 613 patentees disposed of all land, usually within 6-12 months
Approximately 90% of 600 patentees sold or mortgaged allotments
L. S. Bonnin letter regarding fee patent Indian conditions
Bulletin No. 138 from Board of Indian Commissioners requesting field information
Board of Indian Commissioners letter to Commissioner enclosing field service replies
Survey of 35 superintendents, 110 farmers, 13 field clerks regarding fee patents

financial_transactions.description: Sale of patented lands, approximately 90% of recipients sold or mortgaged
Sale of patented lands, approximately 90% of 613 recipients disposed of land
Sale of patented lands, average value $4000 per quarter section
Purchase of automobiles and joy-riding, primary method of squandering proceeds
Purchase of livestock with proceeds by some Shoshone patentees
Mortgaging of patented lands by approximately 90% of recipients across agencies

relationships.subject: white buyers
relationships.context: Purchase of patented lands, often at prices below adequate value

relationships.subject: Competency Commission
relationships.context: Reported on Indian competency leading to patent issuance May 1916

fee_patents.context: Santee Indian, Lake Andes, sold land, working for wages
Santee Indian, Lake Andes, sold land
Yankton Sioux, Wagner, retains land, farming successfully
Yankton Sioux, Wagner, retains land, farming successfully
Shoshone Reservation, good irrigable agricultural lands
Yankton Reservation, 186 issued May 1916, 100+ under Declaration Policy

correspondence.subject: Releasing Indians from Government Supervision
correspondence.action_requested: Review enclosed field service letters regarding fee patents
correspondence.outcome: Enclosed copies of letters from field service men provided

correspondence.subject: Condition of Indians who received patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Provide information on fee patent Indians and views on competency policy

correspondence.subject: Cheyenne & Arapaho fee patent Indians
correspondence.action_requested: Report on condition of fee patent Indians
correspondence.outcome: Report sent with list of names, allotment numbers, land descriptions, acreage and disposition

correspondence.subject: Yankton fee patent policy
correspondence.action_requested: Views on releasing Indians from supervision
correspondence.outcome: Recommended individual consideration, opposed bulk release, opposed reserving home sites

correspondence.subject: Yankton fee patent status and effects
correspondence.action_requested: Information on patents issued and recommendations
correspondence.outcome: Reported 613 patents issued, 90% sold, recommended full citizenship without reservation of land

correspondence.subject: Rosebud fee patent policy and competency standards
correspondence.action_requested: Opinion on competency and patent issuance
correspondence.outcome: Recommended individual examination, opposed bulk release, noted 90% sold or mortgaged

legislative_actions.outcome: Special Act of Congress allowing fee patents for some Yankton Sioux prior to 1916
Declaration of Policy establishing procedure for issuing fee patents 1919-1930
